page: Amazon Web Services r6g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the ARM64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Memory bandwidth remains stable, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 2839.1 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 163.3 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference and single-threaded text processing represent notable performance bottlenecks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the Graviton-based architecture delivers high memory density and efficient multi-core scaling without the premium associated with traditional x86 hardware. This makes the r6g.12xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that scale horizontally. Recommended workloads include in-memory databases, caching fleets such as Redis, open-source database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of system memory, providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, alongside a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, though single-core execution and single-threaded compression tasks are weaker. Memory bandwidth is consistent, with uncached large block reads reaching 163.3 GB/sec. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective alternative to x86 systems for memory-heavy applications. It is best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, multi-threaded compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized virtual server delivering high memory density and strong multi-core performance for scale-out database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM for memory-intensive applications
Delivers 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU performance offset by weaker single-core speeds
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and database operations
